Geddy Lucier
ENVS Senior Thesis - Quantmod Library Testing
10/1/2025

Preamble
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(quantmod)
library(data.table)
library(ggthemes)
library(tidyquant)
library(purrr)
library(alphavantager)


setwd("~/Documents/GitHub/ENVS_Seniorthesis")
getwd()
```
Crude/Brent ticker data prep
```{r}


wti_ticker <- "CL=F"
brent_ticker <- "BZ=F"

start_date <- as.Date("2024-01-01")
end_date <- as.Date("2024-12-31")

# Download historical data
getSymbols(wti_ticker, src = "yahoo", from = start_date, to = end_date, auto.assign = TRUE)
getSymbols(brent_ticker, src = "yahoo", from = start_date, to = end_date, auto.assign = TRUE)

brent <- data.table(Date = index(`BZ=F`), coredata(`BZ=F`))
wti <- data.table(Date = index(`CL=F`), coredata(`CL=F`))

oil_df <- brent %>%
  left_join(wti, by = "Date")

```

Testing - Disaster DF 2024
```{r}

disasters <- read_csv("data/tx_disaster_raw.csv") 

disasters24 <- disasters %>%
  filter(Type != "Drought") %>% 
  mutate(
    Begin.Date = as.Date(`Begin Date`, format = "%B %d, %Y"),
    End.Date   = as.Date(`End Date`, format = "%B %d, %Y")
  ) %>%
  filter(year(Begin.Date) == 2024) %>% # only 2024 disasters
    mutate(
    DisasterDate = map2(Begin.Date, End.Date, seq, by = "day")
  ) %>%
  unnest(DisasterDate) 


#test <- left_join(oil_df, disasters24, by = c("Date" = "DisasterDate"))


oil_disaster <- oil_df %>%
  left_join(disasters24, by = c("Date" = "DisasterDate")) %>% 
  mutate(
    close_diff = `BZ=F.Close` - `CL=F.Close`,
    month = as.numeric(substr(Date, 6, 7)),
    year = lubridate::year(Date),
    season = case_when(
      month %in% c(12, 1, 2) ~ "Winter",
      month %in% c(3, 4, 5)  ~ "Spring",
      month %in% c(6, 7, 8)  ~ "Summer",
      month %in% c(9, 10, 11) ~ "Fall"
    ),
    # Create combined label to distinguish Winter 2023-24 vs Winter 2024-25
    season_year = case_when(
      month == 12 ~ paste0(season, " ", year, "-", year + 1),
      TRUE        ~ paste0(season, " ", year)
    )
  )

  


```


two if else statements: first, if date == startdate, 

maybe do by periods? 



```{r}


ggplot(oil_disaster, aes(x = Date)) +
  # Fill between Brent and WTI only during disaster days
geom_ribbon(
  data = oil_disaster %>% filter(!is.na(Type)),
  aes(ymin = pmin(`CL=F.Close`, `BZ=F.Close`, na.rm = TRUE),
      ymax = pmax(`CL=F.Close`, `BZ=F.Close`, na.rm = TRUE),
      fill = Type,
      group = Event),
  alpha = 0.9
) +
   geom_line(
    data = oil_disaster %>% filter(!is.na(Type)),
    aes(y = pmin(`CL=F.Close`, `BZ=F.Close`, na.rm = TRUE),
        group = Event),
    color = "black", linewidth = 0.3
  ) +
  # Oil price lines
  geom_line(aes(y = `CL=F.Close`, color = "WTI Crude"), na.rm = TRUE) +
  geom_line(aes(y = `BZ=F.Close`, color = "Brent Crude")) +
  labs(
    title = "Brent and WTI Across Texas Weather Disasters 2024",
    y = "Price (USD)",
    x = "Date",
    color = "Crude Type",
    fill  = "Disaster Type"
  ) +
scale_color_manual(values = c(
    "Brent Crude" = "#B44600",  # deep orange
    "WTI Crude"   = "#004488"   # dark navy blue
  )) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "Winter Storm"     = "#4DB3FF",  # bright icy blue
    "Tropical Cyclone" = "#00CC44",  # bright green
    "Severe Storm"     = "#FF3333"   # bright red
  )) +
  theme_bw()
```
```{r}
 oil_disaster %>%
  filter(season_year != "Winter 2024-2025") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Date)) +
  # Fill between Brent and WTI only during disaster days
  geom_ribbon(
    data = . %>% filter(!is.na(Type)),
    aes(ymin = pmin(`CL=F.Close`, `BZ=F.Close`, na.rm = TRUE),
        ymax = pmax(`CL=F.Close`, `BZ=F.Close`, na.rm = TRUE),
        fill = Type,
        group = Event),
    alpha = 0.9
  ) +
  geom_line(
    data = . %>% filter(!is.na(Type)),
    aes(y = pmin(`CL=F.Close`, `BZ=F.Close`, na.rm = TRUE),
        group = Event),
    color = "black", linewidth = 0.3
  ) +
  # Oil price lines
  geom_line(aes(y = `CL=F.Close`, color = "WTI Crude"), na.rm = TRUE) +
  geom_line(aes(y = `BZ=F.Close`, color = "Brent Crude")) +
  labs(
    title = "Brent and WTI Across Texas Weather Disasters 2024",
    y = "Price (USD)",
    x = "Date",
    color = "Crude Type",
    fill  = "Disaster Type"
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(
    "Brent Crude" = "#B44600",  # deep orange
    "WTI Crude"   = "#004488"   # dark navy blue
  )) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "Winter Storm"     = "#4DB3FF",  # bright icy blue
    "Tropical Cyclone" = "#00CC44",  # bright green
    "Severe Storm"     = "#FF3333"   # bright red
  )) +
  theme_bw() +
  facet_wrap(~ season, scales = "free_x")

```


```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

ggplot(oil_disaster, aes(x = Date)) +
  # Highlight full vertical span for disaster periods
  geom_rect(
    data = oil_disaster %>% filter(!is.na(Type)) %>% distinct(Event, Type, Begin.Date, End.Date),
    aes(xmin = Begin.Date, xmax = End.Date,
        ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf, fill = Type),
    inherit.aes = FALSE, alpha = 0.2
  ) +
  # Difference line (Brent - WTI)
  geom_line(aes(y = close_diff, color = "Brent-WTI Spread"), linewidth = 0.8, na.rm = TRUE) +
  labs(
    title = "Brent-WTI Pricing Difference Across Texas Weather Disasters (2024)",
    y = "Price Difference (USD)",
    x = "Date",
    color = "Series",
    fill  = "Disaster Type"
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Brent-WTI Spread" = "black")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "Winter Storm"     = "#4DB3FF",   # bright icy blue
    "Tropical Cyclone" = "#00CC44",   # bright green
    "Severe Storm"     = "#FF3333"    # bright red
  )) +
  theme_bw()
```
  
  
```{r}




 

```
